columns.extraction_plan: The abstract on page 1 states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' The introduction on page 1 further clarifies: 'In ARASENS, patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to double-blind treatment with either darolutamide or placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel.' Therefore, the add-on treatment is Darolutamide.
Extract the number of 'Patients randomly assigned' from Figure 1 on page 3, which is N = 1,306. The text on page 3 also confirms 'a total of 1,306 patients were randomly assigned'.
Extract the median age from Table 1, row 'Age, median, years (range)', column 'Darolutamide (n = 497)' which is 67.0.
Extract the median age from Table 1, row 'Age, median, years (range)', column 'Placebo (n = 508)' which is 67.0.

The number of patients in the control arm (Placebo + docetaxel + ADT) analyzed for the primary end point is 654, as indicated in the 'Analyzed for primary end point' section of the CONSORT flow diagram (Figure 1) on page 3. The 'Trial Design' section on page 2 also states that patients were randomly assigned 1:1 to darolutamide or matched placebo.

plans.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

columns.column_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1
columns.extraction_plan: The treatment arm in the ARASENS trial includes Darolutamide, which is identified as an androgen receptor pathway inhibitor. This is stated in the abstract: 'Darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel' (page 1), and further clarified in the introduction: 'androgen receptor pathway inhibitor was evaluated in several phase III trials (PEACE-1, ENZAMET, and ARASENS)' (page 1), and in the discussion: 'adding an androgen receptor inhibitor (darolutamide) to ADT and docetaxel' (page 11).

The CONSORT flow diagram (FIG 1) on page 3 shows that 655 patients were randomly assigned to the 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT' arm. Footnote 'c' clarifies that one patient assigned to the placebo arm received darolutamide, resulting in 654 patients analyzed for the primary end point in the placebo arm. Table 1 on page 5, under 'All Patients', also lists 'Placebo (n = 654)'.

The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' The 'Trial Design' section on page 2 further clarifies: 'Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel.' Figure 1 on page 3 shows that 651 patients received allocated treatment in the Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT arm. Therefore, 651 (100%) patients in the treatment arm received docetaxel.

The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' The 'Trial Design' section on page 2 further clarifies: 'Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel.' Figure 1 on page 3 shows that 651 patients received allocated treatment in the Placebo + docetaxel + ADT arm. Therefore, 651 (100%) patients in the control arm received docetaxel.

The PDF mentions 'median treatment duration' on page 4 and page 11 (e.g., 'median treatment duration in ARASENS was 41 months for darolutamide plus ADT and docetaxel versus 34.1 months in the docetaxel subgroup of the abiraterone group in PEACE-1'), but it does not explicitly state the 'Median Follow-Up Duration (mo)' for the ARASENS trial. I have reviewed the abstract, methods, results, and discussion sections, as well as all tables and figures (Tables 1, 2, 3, 4, A1 and Figures 1, 2, 3, A1) on pages 1-18, but this specific metric is not reported.
Extract '41 months' from the text on page 11, which states 'median treatment duration in ARASENS was 41 months for darolutamide plus ADT and docetaxel'.

The median on-treatment duration for the placebo arm of the ARASENS trial is not explicitly reported for the overall population. Page 4 discusses duration of treatment for subgroups (e.g., high-volume: 15.3 months, low-volume: 26.5 months for placebo), but an overall median for the control arm is not provided. Page 11 mentions the darolutamide arm's duration but not the placebo arm's for ARASENS.

Extract the value 'NR' (Not Reached) from Table 2, row 'Time to CRPC', column 'All Patients' -> 'Darolutamide (n = 651)'. The table reports 'Time to CRPC' as a secondary efficacy endpoint, which is considered equivalent to PFS in this context.

Extract the value '19.1' from Table 2, row 'Time to CRPC', column 'All Patients' -> 'Placebo (n = 654)'. The table reports 'Time to CRPC' as a secondary efficacy endpoint, which is considered equivalent to PFS in this context.

Extract the value 'NR' (Not Reached) from Table 2, row 'Time to CRPC', column 'High Volumeª' -> 'Darolutamide (n = 497)'. The table reports 'Time to CRPC' as a secondary efficacy endpoint, which is considered equivalent to PFS in this context.

Extract the value '16.4' from Table 2, row 'Time to CRPC', column 'High Volumeª' -> 'Placebo (n = 508)'. The table reports 'Time to CRPC' as a secondary efficacy endpoint, which is considered equivalent to PFS in this context.

Extract the value 'NR' (Not Reached) from Table 2, row 'Time to CRPC', column 'Low Volumeª' -> 'Darolutamide (n = 154)'. The table reports 'Time to CRPC' as a secondary efficacy endpoint, which is considered equivalent to PFS in this context.

Extract the value '25.8' from Table 2, row 'Time to CRPC', column 'Low Volumeª' -> 'Placebo (n = 146)'. The table reports 'Time to CRPC' as a secondary efficacy endpoint, which is considered equivalent to PFS in this context.

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: Sum the N and % values for 'Grade 3', 'Grade 4', and 'Grade 5 (death)' under the 'Worst grade' section for 'Darolutamide (n = 652)' in the 'All Patients' column of Table 3 on page 9. Grade 3: 248 (38.0%), Grade 4: 183 (28.1%), Grade 5 (death): 27 (4.1%). Total N = 248 + 183 + 27 = 458. Total % = 38.0 + 28.1 + 4.1 = 70.2%. Value: 458 (70.2%).

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Control
columns.extraction_plan: Sum the N and % values for 'Grade 3', 'Grade 4', and 'Grade 5 (death)' under the 'Worst grade' section for 'Placebo (n = 650)' in the 'All Patients' column of Table 3 on page 9. Grade 3: 232 (35.7%), Grade 4: 181 (27.8%), Grade 5 (death): 26 (4.0%). Total N = 232 + 181 + 26 = 439. Total % = 35.7 + 27.8 + 4.0 = 67.5%. Value: 439 (67.5%).

Extract 'ARASENS' from the title of the article on page 1: 'Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer by Disease Volume and Risk Subgroups in the Phase III ARASENS Trial'.

David Crawford, MD; Neal Shore, MD7; Evgeny Kopyltsov, MD; Arash Rezazadeh Kalebasty, MD; Martin Bögemann, MD10; Dingwei Ye, MD11; Felipe Cruz, MD, PhD12; Hiroyoshi Suzuki, MD, PhD13; Shivani Kapur, MD14; Shankar Srinivasan, PhD15; Frank Verholen, MD16; Iris Kuss, MD17; Heikki Joensuu, MD18; and Matthew R. The document is a full publication, indicated by 'original reports' in the sidebar on page 1 and the extensive content (18 pages) including detailed methods, results, and discussion sections.

Extract 'Phase III' from the title of the article on page 1: '...in the Phase III ARASENS Trial'. This is further confirmed in the 'Trial Design' section on page 2: 'ARASENS was a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase III trial...'

This is a follow-up/subgroup analysis. The title on page 1 specifies 'by Disease Volume and Risk Subgroups'. The 'METHODS' section on page 2 states: 'We conducted these subgroup analyses post hoc to determine if specific subgroups of patients by disease volume or risk would achieve greater benefit...'. Additionally, page 3 references the original ARASENS publication from 2022: 'From N Engl J Med, Smith MR, Hussain M, Saad F, et al, Darolutamide and Survival in Metastatic, Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer, 386(12), 1132-1142,9 Copyright© 2022...'

The 'abstract' on page 1 states, 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' The 'Trial Design' section on page 2 further clarifies, 'Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel.' This indicates two arms. Figure 1 on page 3, the CONSORT flow diagram, visually confirms the two arms: 'Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT' and 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT'.

PFS Rate (%) for the overall treatment group is not reported in the PDF. The document discusses Overall Survival (OS) and Time to Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer (CRPC) as primary and secondary endpoints, respectively, but does not provide Progression-Free Survival (PFS) rates. I checked the abstract on page 1, the 'Study Assessments' section on page 3, and Table 2 on page 8 which lists secondary efficacy endpoints, and Figure 3 on page 7 which shows time to CRPC, but none report PFS rates.

PFS Rate (%) for the overall control group is not reported in the PDF. Extract 'NE' from the 'Overall' row under 'Darolutamide + ADT + Docetaxel' in the 'Median' column of Figure A1 (A) on page 16. Figure 2 (A) on page 6 also shows the Darolutamide curve not reaching the median.

Extract '42.4' from the 'Overall' row under 'Placebo + ADT + Docetaxel' in the 'Median' column of Figure A1 (A) on page 16. Figure 2 (A) on page 6 also shows the median for the placebo group at 42.4 months.

The text on page 4 states 'Median survival was not reached in the darolutamide group' for high-volume disease. Figure 2 (A) on page 6 also indicates 'NE' (Not Estimable) for the Darolutamide arm in the high-volume subgroup. Extract 'NE'.

The text on page 4 states 'Median survival... was 42.4 months (95% CI, 39.7 to 46.0 months) in the placebo group' for high-volume disease. Figure 2 (A) on page 6 also indicates '42.4 months' for the Placebo arm in the high-volume subgroup. Extract '42.4'.

The text on page 4 states 'Median survival was not reached in either treatment group for low-volume patients'. Figure 2 (B) on page 6 and Figure A1 (B) on page 17 also indicate 'NE' (Not Estimable) for the Darolutamide arm in the low-volume subgroup. Extract 'NE'.

The text on page 4 states 'Median survival was not reached in either treatment group for low-volume patients'. Figure 2 (B) on page 6 and Figure A1 (B) on page 17 also indicate 'NE' (Not Estimable) for the Placebo arm in the low-volume subgroup. Extract 'NE'.

The PDF provides 'M1c, visceral metastases ± LN or bone' in Table 1 (page 5) for the Darolutamide arm, which includes 111 patients (17.0%) across high-volume and low-volume subgroups. However, it does not specifically break down visceral metastases into 'Liver' metastases. Therefore, the exact number and percentage for liver metastases in the treatment arm are not reported.

The PDF provides 'M1c, visceral metastases ± LN or bone' in Table 1 (page 5) for the Placebo arm, which includes 118 patients (18.0%) across high-volume and low-volume subgroups. However, it does not specifically break down visceral metastases into 'Liver' metastases. Therefore, the exact number and percentage for liver metastases in the control arm are not reported.

The PDF provides 'M1c, visceral metastases ± LN or bone' in Table 1 (page 5) for the Darolutamide arm, which includes 111 patients (17.0%) across high-volume and low-volume subgroups. However, it does not specifically break down visceral metastases into 'Lungs' metastases. Therefore, the exact number and percentage for lung metastases in the treatment arm are not reported.

Extract 'M1b, bone metastases ± LN' from Table 1 (page 5). For the Darolutamide arm, sum the counts from 'High Volume' (386) and 'Low Volume' (131) subgroups to get a total of 517 patients. The total number of Darolutamide patients is 651 (497 in High Volume + 154 in Low Volume). Calculate the percentage as (517/651) * 100 = 79.4%. Value: 517 (79.4%).

Extract 'M1b, bone metastases ± LN' from Table 1 (page 5). For the Placebo arm, sum the counts from 'High Volume' (390) and 'Low Volume' (131) subgroups to get a total of 521 patients. The total number of Placebo patients is 654 (508 in High Volume + 146 in Low Volume). Calculate the percentage as (521/654) * 100 = 79.7%. Value: 521 (79.7%).

Extract 'M1a, nonregional LN only' from Table 1 (page 5). For the Darolutamide arm, sum the counts from 'High Volume' (0) and 'Low Volume' (23) subgroups to get a total of 23 patients. The total number of Darolutamide patients is 651. Calculate the percentage as (23/651) * 100 = 3.5%. Value: 23 (3.5%).

Extract 'M1a, nonregional LN only' from Table 1 (page 5). For the Placebo arm, sum the counts from 'High Volume' (0) and 'Low Volume' (15) subgroups to get a total of 15 patients. The total number of Placebo patients is 654. Calculate the percentage as (15/654) * 100 = 2.3%. Value: 15 (2.3%).

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: The 'Synchronous' mode of metastases is defined as 'De novo mHSPC' on page 2. The total count for 'De novo mHSPC' in the Darolutamide (Treatment) arm is calculated by summing the 'De novo mHSPC' counts from the 'High Volume' (432) and 'Low Volume' (126) subgroups in Table 1 (page 5). This sum is 432 + 126 = 558. The total number of patients in the Darolutamide arm is 651 (from Table 1 header). The percentage is (558 / 651) * 100 = 85.7%.

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous | Control
columns.extraction_plan: The 'Synchronous' mode of metastases is defined as 'De novo mHSPC' on page 2. The total count for 'De novo mHSPC' in the Placebo (Control) arm is calculated by summing the 'De novo mHSPC' counts from the 'High Volume' (445) and 'Low Volume' (121) subgroups in Table 1 (page 5). This sum is 445 + 121 = 566. The total number of patients in the Placebo arm is 654 (from Table 1 header). The percentage is (566 / 654) * 100 = 86.5%.

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronous | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: The 'Metachronous' mode of metastases is defined as 'Recurrent mHSPC' on page 2. The total count for 'Recurrent mHSPC' in the Darolutamide (Treatment) arm is calculated by summing the 'Recurrent mHSPC' counts from the 'High Volume' (58) and 'Low Volume' (28) subgroups in Table 1 (page 5). This sum is 58 + 28 = 86. The total number of patients in the Darolutamide arm is 651 (from Table 1 header). The percentage is (86 / 651) * 100 = 13.2%.

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronous | Control
columns.extraction_plan: The 'Metachronous' mode of metastases is defined as 'Recurrent mHSPC' on page 2. The total count for 'Recurrent mHSPC' in the Placebo (Control) arm is calculated by summing the 'Recurrent mHSPC' counts from the 'High Volume' (59) and 'Low Volume' (23) subgroups in Table 1 (page 5). This sum is 59 + 23 = 82. The total number of patients in the Placebo arm is 654 (from Table 1 header). The percentage is (82 / 654) * 100 = 12.5%.

The Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire was used to measure "time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms" as a secondary efficacy end point. This is stated on page 3 under "Study Assessments". Table 2 on page 8 reports the results for "Time to worsening disease-related symptoms".

Quality of Life was assessed via secondary endpoints including 'Time to pain progression' (measured by Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire) and 'Time to worsening disease-related symptoms' (measured by Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate symptom index 17-item questionnaire), as described on page 3 under 'Study Assessments'. The results for these endpoints, including median months, number of patients with events, and HR (95% CI) for Darolutamide and Placebo groups across various subgroups (High Volume, Low Volume, High Risk, Low Risk, and All Patients), are presented in Table 2 on page 8. Additionally, 'Time to opioid use " 7 days' is also reported in Table 2, which is a related measure of quality of life.
columns.column_name_raw: Time to pain progression; Time to worsening disease-related symptoms; Time to opioid use ">= 7 days

columns.extraction_plan: The PDF does not report 'Time to PSA progression' (TTPSA) for the treatment arm. Table 2 (page 8) lists secondary efficacy endpoints such as 'Time to CRPC' and 'Time to pain progression', but not TTPSA. The 'Secondary Efficacy End Points' section on page 4 and 'Study Assessments' on page 3 also do not mention TTPSA.

The PDF does not report 'Time to PSA progression' (TTPSA) for the control arm. Table 2 (page 8) lists secondary efficacy endpoints such as 'Time to CRPC' and 'Time to pain progression', but not TTPSA. The 'Secondary Efficacy End Points' section on page 4 and 'Study Assessments' on page 3 also do not mention TTPSA.

The secondary efficacy end points are explicitly listed in the 'Study Assessments' section on page 3. These include: time to castration-resistant prostate cancer, time to pain progression, symptomatic skeletal event-free survival, time to first symptomatic-skeletal event, time to initiation of subsequent systemic antineoplastic therapy, time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms, and time to initiation of opioid use for 7 or more days. Table 2 on page 8 provides the results for these secondary endpoints.

The document reports median overall survival (OS) as 'Not Estimable' (NE) for the Darolutamide treatment group in the overall population (Figure A1, page 16, 'Overall' row). Specific OS rates (%) are not provided; instead, Kaplan-Meier curves are shown in Figure 2 (page 6) and median OS values are reported.

The document reports median overall survival (OS) as 42.4 months for the Placebo control group in the overall population (Figure A1, page 16, 'Overall' row). Specific OS rates (%) are not provided; instead, Kaplan-Meier curves are shown in Figure 2 (page 6) and median OS values are reported.

Specific OS rates (%) are not provided; instead, Kaplan-Meier curves and median OS values are reported.

The document reports median overall survival (OS) as 42.4 months (95% CI, 39.7 to 46.0 months) for the Placebo control group in the high-volume disease subgroup (Figure 2A, page 6; Figure A1, page 16, 'Extent of disease (eCRF)' section, 'High Volume' row). Specific OS rates (%) are not provided; instead, Kaplan-Meier curves and median OS values are reported.

Extract the number and percentage of patients with ECOG PS 0 for the Darolutamide (Treatment) arm by summing the values from the 'High Volume' (343) and 'Low Volume' (123) subgroups in Table 1 on page 5. Total N = 343 + 123 = 466. Total Darolutamide patients = 497 + 154 = 651. Percentage = (466/651)*100 = 71.6%.

Extract the number and percentage of patients with ECOG PS 0 for the Placebo (Control) arm by summing the values from the 'High Volume' (349) and 'Low Volume' (113) subgroups in Table 1 on page 5. Total N = 349 + 113 = 462. Total Placebo patients = 508 + 146 = 654. Percentage = (462/654)*100 = 70.6%.

Extract the number and percentage of patients with ECOG PS 1 for the Darolutamide (Treatment) arm by summing the values from the 'High Volume' (154) and 'Low Volume' (31) subgroups in Table 1 on page 5. Total N = 154 + 31 = 185. Total Darolutamide patients = 651. Percentage = (185/651)*100 = 28.4%. The table only reports ECOG PS 0 and 1, and the sum of PS 0 and PS 1 accounts for all patients in the Darolutamide arm (466 + 185 = 651), indicating no patients had PS 2 or higher. Therefore, PS 1-2 is equivalent to PS 1.

Extract the number and percentage of patients with ECOG PS 1 for the Placebo (Control) arm by summing the values from the 'High Volume' (157) and 'Low Volume' (33) subgroups in Table 1 on page 5. Total N = 157 + 33 = 190. Total Placebo patients = 654. Percentage = (190/654)*100 = 29.1%. The table only reports ECOG PS 0 and 1, and the sum of PS 0, PS 1, and missing values accounts for all patients in the Placebo arm (462 + 190 + 2 = 654), indicating no patients had PS 2 or higher. Therefore, PS 1-2 is equivalent to PS 1.

The abstract on page 1 states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' The introduction on page 1 further clarifies: 'In ARASENS, patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to double-blind treatment with either darolutamide or placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel.' The Methods section on page 2 confirms: 'Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel.' Therefore, Treatment Arm 1 is Darolutamide + ADT + Docetaxel.

The 'RESULTS Patient Characteristics' section on page 3 states: 'As previously reported, a total of 1,306 patients were randomly assigned, and 1,305 patients were included in'. Figure 1, the CONSORT flow diagram, shows 'Patients randomly assigned (N = 1,306)' and the total for 'Analyzed for primary end point' as 651 (Darolutamide) + 654 (Placebo) = 1305. The value 1305 represents the total participants included in the primary analysis.

The abstract on page 1 states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' The Methods section on page 2 further clarifies: 'Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel.' Figure 1 on page 3, the CONSORT flow diagram, explicitly labels the two arms as 'Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT' and 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT'.

The number of patients in the Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT treatment arm is reported as 651 in the CONSORT flow diagram (Figure 1, page 3) under 'Patients randomly assigned' and 'Analyzed for primary end point'. This is further confirmed in Table 1 (page 5) under the 'All Patients' column for 'Darolutamide'.

The ARASENS trial involved two treatment arms: 'Darolutamide + androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel' and 'Placebo + androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel'. This is stated in the abstract on page 1 and the methods section on page 2.

The ARASENS trial involved two treatment arms: 'Darolutamide + ADT + Docetaxel' and 'Placebo + ADT + Docetaxel'. This is explicitly stated in the abstract on page 1, the methods section on page 2, and shown in the CONSORT flow diagram (Figure 1, page 3) and all subsequent tables (e.g., Table 1, page 5).

The trial is named ARASENS, as stated in the title on page 1: 'Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer by Disease Volume and Risk Subgroups in the Phase III ARASENS Trial'. The abstract on page 1 and the Methods section on page 2 also refer to 'the ARASENS trial'. The Clinical Trial Information on page 12 provides the identifier 'NCT02799602'.

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous
columns.extraction_plan: The PDF reports on 'de novo mHSPC' (synchronous) patients. This is found in the 'Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis' section of Table 1 on page 5, and also discussed in the abstract on page 1 and methods on page 2. Figure A1 on pages 16-17 also includes 'De novo' as a subgroup.

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume

Figures 2 and 3 (pages 6-7) and Figure A1 (pages 16-17) also present data by high-volume disease.

The 'All Patients' column in Table 1 (page 5) does not provide Gleason score data split by treatment arm. The value is derived by summing the 'Gleason score at initial diagnosis,d No. (%)' for '<8' from the 'High Volume' (96) and 'Low Volume' (26) Darolutamide subgroups, totaling 122 patients. The percentage is calculated based on the total Darolutamide patients (651): (122/651)*100 = 18.7%.

The 'All Patients' column in Table 1 (page 5) does not provide Gleason score data split by treatment arm. The value is derived by summing the 'Gleason score at initial diagnosis,d No. (%)' for '<8' from the 'High Volume' (87) and 'Low Volume' (31) Placebo subgroups, totaling 118 patients. The percentage is calculated based on the total Placebo patients (654): (118/654)*100 = 18.0%.

The 'All Patients' column in Table 1 (page 5) does not provide Gleason score data split by treatment arm. The value is derived by summing the 'Gleason score at initial diagnosis,d No. (%)' for '≥ 8' from the 'High Volume' (381) and 'Low Volume' (124) Darolutamide subgroups, totaling 505 patients. The percentage is calculated based on the total Darolutamide patients (651): (505/651)*100 = 77.6%.

The 'All Patients' column in Table 1 (page 5) does not provide Gleason score data split by treatment arm.
